@graph.description: This experiment executes a single file download with client-server pairs of different QUIC implementations. The filesize, the implementations, and a bandwidth limit can be set to multiple values to execute the measurements with all combinations of the parameters. The amount of repetitions can also be set to repeat the same combination n times to ensure stable results. An inbuilt evaluation script aggregates results and creates a simple plot that compares different QUIC implementations.
@graph.name: Performance Evaluation of QUIC Implementations with Limited Bandwidth

build
@graph.description: Install Rust and Go to then build all QUIC implementations from source.

@graph.name: build
@graph.description: Install Rust and Go to then build all QUIC implementations from source.

@graph.name: run-server
@graph.description: Start a QUIC server with a given library. In advance, a testfile and a certificate is generated. If set, a bandwidth limit is applied with Linux tc. After a client signalizes a finished transfer, the server is terminated.

@graph.name: run-server_run00
@graph.description: In each iteration, the QUIC client downloads a testfile from the QUIC server. All combinations for all values of implementation, bandwidth and filesize are run at least once, depending on the amount of repetitions set. For each run, the duration as well as logs from the respective endpoint are stored.

@graph.name: run-client
@graph.description: Start a QUIC client to download a file from a QUIC server. After a complete download, the file is hashed and the resulting value is stored to a file.

@graph.name: run-client_run00
@graph.description: In each iteration, the QUIC client downloads a testfile from the QUIC server.
